title: Rei in EoE poster and final scene
display_title: Rei in EoE poster and final scene
wikitext:
[]: Rei in EoE poster and final scene             Quote Post by cd603 » Sat Oct 02, 2010 9:51 pm I was looking around on Wikipedia when I stumbled across this: As you can clearly see, I've circled Rei standing above the water in green. Now for the really creepy part: By looking at this image you can clearly see Rei STANDING ABOVE THE WATER IN THE EXACT SAME OUTFIT! Someone care to explain this????

[]: Re: An intersting discovery (possible spoilers)             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 02, 2010 9:54 pm cd603 wrote: Someone care to explain this????

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Oct 02, 2010 9:57 pm Hmm, I never noticed that in the poster.. but I guess I never really spent the time to analyze it.

[]: Re: An intersting discovery (possible spoilers)             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Oct 02, 2010 9:59 pm the Red water is what is left of all the people that were tanged during 3I, so it contains all their souls. Rei or that ghost/spirit Rei thing it the shot with Shinji and Asuka on the Shore is short of watching over them, while the faded out Rei on the cover is watching from the surface over all the tanged people's souls. So in a way she is doing two jobs, watching over the people who decide to return to human form after 3I and watching over the soul/spirits of those who reject human form after 3I. and I have no idea why I am posting this because it is just going to be ignored

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 02, 2010 10:24 pm Old news is old. (You don't need to mark NGE or EoE as being spoilers by the way. When you join the forums it's assumed you know you'll be spoiled as the Rules state.) Lilith becomes Rei (instead of Rei becoming Lilith), but everybody is reverted to LCL due to Third Impact. It makes sense that she's standing outside of the LCL Sea while in the EoE poster the female cast is within the sea. And Rei being floating above the LCL Sea in either image is probably meant to invoke Lilith's quantum physics.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Sun Oct 03, 2010 1:19 am Ok I get that Rei is Lilith or whatever, but what confused me is that I thought there was actually bodies lying under the water rather than it just being LCL. I guess in a way the bodies are still there, but we can't see them. (btw, thank you to whoever renamed this thread!)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Oct 03, 2010 1:45 am cd603 wrote: Ok I get that Rei is Lilith or whatever, but what confused me is that I thought there was actually bodies lying under the water rather than it just being LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Oct 03, 2010 1:47 am CD603: No problem. And yeah, the bodies (of the female cast) in the poster imply how all the cast is LCL in the film, metaphysically communicating during pre and actual Instrumentality. Hope that makes sense.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Sun Oct 03, 2010 3:00 pm NemZ wrote: Then again, maybe there are? It's not like the red ocean looks especially translucent from outside.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Oct 03, 2010 3:01 pm They're souls floating in the red sea. Also, I never noticed Rei in that poster. That's awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Oct 03, 2010 3:38 pm NemZ wrote: Then again, maybe there are? It's not like the red ocean looks especially translucent from outside.

[]:             Quote Post by Seph » Sun Oct 03, 2010 4:10 pm Guys; It's a poster. It's meant to create excitement for something you don't know anything about yet. Showing most of the cast "drowning" in a red sea is a very good way to do that, but that doesn't mean it's canon. Rei being there is, but that's only because we can back it up with EoE and NGE Ep. 1.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Oct 03, 2010 5:24 pm It's an interesting concept though, potentially useful in a suitably dark fanfic.

[]:             Quote Post by Da Games Elite » Sun Oct 03, 2010 5:37 pm In all fairness, it's an unsurprising yet awesome discovery. Yeah, the red poster is the LCL sea and all that, and any theory that this is what the LCL sea looks like the bottom would work...save for one little flaw... Asuka's in that picture curled up in a fetal position. ;P

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Sun Oct 03, 2010 7:13 pm Da Games Elite wrote: Asuka's in that picture curled up in a fetal position. ;P

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sun Oct 03, 2010 7:16 pm IT'S PEOPLE! LCL IS PEOPLE! But yeah, that's creepy as hell. Maybe there really are bodies under the sea. That certainly would be weird after Shinji floated to the shore.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Oct 03, 2010 7:22 pm If Asuka wasn't affected for 3I, then she along with Shinji, Ritsuko, Misato and Rei wouldn't have been communicating during that pre-Instrumentality sequence (from sandbox scene up until the kitchen scene strangling). And I doubt there are bodies in the sea, the poster is just there stylistically to imply how everybody's been tanged. (I that poster at any rate. It's so awesome.)

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Sun Oct 03, 2010 7:45 pm Da Games Elite wrote: In all fairness, it's an unsurprising yet awesome discovery. Yeah, the red poster is the LCL sea and all that, and any theory that this is what the LCL sea looks like the bottom would work...save for one little flaw... Asuka's in that picture curled up in a fetal position. ;P

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Oct 03, 2010 8:33 pm Or that everyone comes back.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Oct 03, 2010 8:50 pm There is that shot during the Shinji/Rei/Kaworu discussion showing bodies appearing around them, floating. Yeah, I do think it's just symbolic of the return that Shinji is hoping for, but it does potentially suggest that people will rematerialize out in the sea and have to just wash up on shore or swim there if they wake up before that. Is the ocean breathable now though, are plenty of people going to come back only to drown?
